Good. Now I have all the data I need. Let me calculate the target dimensions (max rendered × 1.1, rounded up) for each image and run the conversions:

**Max rendered across viewports:**
- gallery-1..8: desktop wins (larger), except gallery images are 135×225 → 149×248px
- timeline-40/50/60s: mobile 309×309 wins → 340×340
- how-it-works-1..4: mobile 420×340 → 462×374
- buy-box-1..6: mobile 462×462 → 508×508
- guide images: mobile 425×425 → 468×468
- care-doctors: desktop 308×356 → 339×392
- longevity-benefits: desktop 719×757 → 791×833

input.command:
DIR="/Users/jbiggs/code/buy-hh/buy-honehealth-com/longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages/src/static/media/images/opt-longevity"

convert_webp() {
  local name="$1" w="$2" h="$3"
  convert "$DIR/${name}.png" -resize "${w}x${h}" -quality 80 -define webp:method=6 "$DIR/${name}.webp"
  echo "✓ $name: $(identify -format '%wx%h' "$DIR/${name}.webp")"
}

DIR="/Users/jbiggs/code/buy-hh/buy-honehealth-com/longevity-lp/themes/HoneLandingPages/src/static/media/images/opt-longevity"

for f in "$DIR"/*.png; do
  filename=$(basename "$f" .png)
  dims=$(echo "$filename" | grep -oE '[0-9]+x[0-9]+$')
  base=$(echo "$filename" | sed "s/_${dims}$//")
  magick "$f" -resize "${dims}" -quality 80 -define webp:method=6 "$DIR/${base}.webp"
  echo "✓ ${base}.webp → $(magick identify -format '%wx%h' "$DIR/${base}.webp")"
done
